statement.p: The concentration of a drug in a patients blood stream, in milligrams per liter, can be modeled by the function C(t)={\frac{4t}{t^{2}+8}}\text{,} where t is the number of hours since the drug is injected. Answer the following question with technology.
hours since injection, the drugs concentration is at the maximum value of milligrams per liter.

solution.p: To locate the points on C(t) where the y-value is maximum, we use technology to graph the function C(t)={\frac{4t}{t^{2}+8}}\text{,} and then locate its maximum value.

The point with the maximum y-value is (2.83,0.71)\text{.} It implies the drug concentration in the blood stream will reach the maximum value of 0.71 milligrams per liter approximately 2.83 hours since injection.
